title: Codex Turn Lifecycle — State Evolution
note: IEC 60848 Grafcet modelling the observable state transitions in a single Codex API turn, from request building through compaction.

steps.name: Idle
steps.description: Session awaiting user input. WS may be connected (warm) or disconnected.

steps.name: Auth Resolved
steps.description: OAuth token valid, originator set, installation_id available.

steps.name: Request Built
steps.description: ResponsesApiRequest body assembled: instructions, input, tools, reasoning, text, client_metadata, context_management.

steps.name: Delta Computed
steps.description: Incremental input delta calculated against previous request. If WS session invalid or request shape changed, falls back to full input.

steps.name: Transport Selected
steps.description: Transport chosen: WS (primary), HTTP SSE (fallback), or WebRTC (realtime). Headers attached including x-codex-window-id.

steps.name: Request Dispatched
steps.description: Request sent on selected transport. Awaiting first response frame.

steps.name: Streaming
steps.description: Response events streaming: text deltas, tool calls, reasoning items. Token usage accumulating.

steps.name: Turn Complete
steps.description: Response stream ended. Final token usage known. response_id captured for continuation.

steps.name: Compaction Triggered
steps.description: Token usage exceeds compact_threshold. Compaction initiated (remote or inline).

steps.name: Compaction Complete
steps.description: History replaced with compacted summary. WS session reset. Window generation advanced.

steps.name: Error / Fallback
steps.description: Transport error, auth failure, or rate limit. May trigger WS→HTTP fallback or retry.

transitions.condition: User input received ∧ auth token valid

actions: Compute input delta if WS session valid
Initiate compaction (remote if OpenAI, inline otherwise)

transitions.condition: Mid-stream stall timeout ∨ WS close ∨ rate_limit_exceeded

actions: WS→HTTP fallback if WS error (sticky per session)

parallel_branches.name: Subagent Fork
parallel_branches.description: During S6, tool calls may spawn subagent turns. Each subagent follows its own S0→S7 lifecycle with inherited auth but separate thread_id.
parallel_branches.notes: Subagent gets x-codex-parent-thread-id = parent conversation_id, x-openai-subagent header, separate window_generation

parallel_branches.name: Inter-Agent Mailbox
parallel_branches.description: NEW: Agents can send InterAgentCommunication via Mailbox. Receiver checks pending mails before/during turn. Preemption possible after reasoning/commentary items.
